Voice control — after enabling in the app, say 'Hey assistant' followed by commands like 'Play', 'Pause', 'Next', 'Volume Up/Down', 'Ambient ON/OFF', 'Noise Canceling ON/OFF', 'Spotify', 'Assistant', or 'End Call'; incoming calls also support 'Accept' or 'Reject' without the wake phrase. 2. Multipoint Bluetooth connection — pair and switch seamlessly between two devices (e.g., smartphone and laptop). 3. Ambient awareness & ANC mode cycling — press the left earbud to toggle between modes. 4. Hardware reset — press and hold for 10 seconds if earbuds become unresponsive. 5. App-based customization — use the official app to customize button actions, enable hands-free voice control, browse advanced features, and update firmware for optimal performance.

If your earphones won't work, you can try a hardware reset first: press and hold the earbuds for 10 seconds to restart them. This is recommended only if they’re unresponsive or locked up (e.g., flashing red and blue lights). For a factory reset — which erases all settings — ensure the earbuds are disconnected from Bluetooth and removed from the charging case, then press and hold either earbud for 6 seconds. After resetting, you may need to re-pair them with your device via Bluetooth.

关于耳机的维护与保养政策，具体如下： 1. 清洁方法：请使用柔软干布或纸巾擦拭耳塞及充电盒，切勿使用任何强效化学清洁剂、溶剂或湿布；清洁前请确保耳塞已完全干燥。 2. 存放要求：不使用时，请将耳机存放在阴凉、干燥处，避免阳光直射；切勿暴露于高温（＞100°F / 37.8°C）、严寒或潮湿环境中。 3. 使用注意事项： - 切勿拉扯或猛拽充电线缆，插拔时请轻柔操作； - 切勿将耳机浸水、置于水中或靠近水源； - 切勿让儿童单独使用或玩耍本产品（含小部件，有窒息风险）； - 切勿自行拆解、维修或改装设备；如需服务，请联系授权售后服务中心。 4. 环保处置：请依据当地法规对耳机及包装材料进行分类回收；锂电池不可随意丢弃，建议送至指定电子废弃物回收点或联系制造商获取回收指导。 5. 防护等级：本耳机具备IP55级防汗防尘能力，但仍需避免长期接触大量汗水或灰尘。 6. 其他提示：请定期通过配套App更新固件以保障最佳性能与体验；若出现异常发热、异味、变色或充电异常，请立即停止使用并联系客服。

Based on the three observing views (front, side/back, and interface-level functional views) described in the documentation, the eReader should have the following buttons and interfaces: 1. **Front View Buttons & Interfaces**: - Home/ESC button: for exiting current operation or returning to previous interface; - Prev/Next Page buttons: for page navigation; - Navigation/Menu button: 4-way directional control (up/down/left/right) + menu/confirm function; - Zoom In/Out button: to adjust JPEG zoom or eBook font size; - Rotate button: to switch between horizontal/vertical display orientation; - Play/Pause button: for music or MIC recording control; - Power button: long-press (3–5 sec) to power on/off; - Volume Up (+) and Volume Down (–) buttons: for audio level adjustment; - 7″ TFT LCD Display Screen (800×480 px): primary user interface showing menus, content, battery indicator, USB connection status, etc. 2. **Side/Back View Interfaces**: - 3.5 mm Earphone Jack: for private audio output; - USB Port (Mini USB): for charging and computer data transfer; - Micro SD card reader slot: supports up to 16GB SDHC/TF cards; - Reset On/Off switch: located on the back next to the card reader, for factory reset; - Speaker (0.5W): built-in audio output; 3. **Software Interface (UI) Elements**: - Main Menu screen with icons/options: "Browser History", "eBook", "Music", "Video", "Photo", "Record", "Explorer", "Settings"; - USB Connection mode interface: auto-appears when connected to PC, showing two removable disks (internal memory + SD card); - Battery indicator (upper right of Main Menu); - Charging icon shown during USB charging; - Status prompts (e.g., 'USB Connection', error messages in Troubleshooting section).

The 'Main Menu' serves as the central navigation hub of the eReader, allowing access to core functions such as eBook reading, browser mode, bookmarks (Save/Load/Del Mark), page jump, brightness/color settings, flip time, and screen rotation. It is entered via the 'M' button during eBook mode or accessed directly from the home interface using the Navigation/Menu button. The 'Browser History' displays a list of recently browsed books — selecting an entry with the 'M' button resumes reading from the exact last page viewed in that book. Note: if a book was stored on a Micro SD card and later removed, its record remains in Browser History but cannot be opened.
When in eBook mode, pressing the "M" button opens a menu with multiple functions: "Page Jump", "Save Mark", "Load Mark", "Del Mark", "Browser Mode", "Flip Time", "Brightness", and "Set Color".

To listen to music on your eReader, follow these steps: 1. Transfer supported audio files (MP3, WMA, WAV, ACC, ECT) to the device by connecting it to your computer via USB cable — it will appear as a removable disk; drag and drop files into the internal memory or Micro SD card (if inserted). 2. From the main menu, open the "Music" section (refer to page 8 of the manual). 3. Select your desired audio file and press "M" to enter music play mode. 4. Use the physical buttons to control playback: Press "Play/Pause" to start/stop; use "Up/Down" keys to adjust volume; hold "Left/Right" keys to rewind/forward; press "Left/Right" once to skip to previous/next track. You can also listen to music while reading an eBook or browsing photos. Note: A 3.5 mm earphone jack is provided for private listening (earphones not included), and the device has a built-in speaker.

Yes, this eReader supports voice recording. To use this feature: 1. Go to the Main Menu; 2. Select "Record"; 3. Press the "M" button to enter voice record mode. For more details, refer to the "Voice Recording" section on page 13 of the user manual.

To play video on your eReader, follow these steps: 1. Go to the "Video" section from the main menu. 2. Press the "M" key to enter Video mode. 3. Select the desired video file (supported formats: AVI, RMVB, MPEG2, MOV, MP4) and press "M" again to start playback. Make sure your video files are copied to the device via USB connection — connect the eReader to your computer using the USB cable, then drag and drop compatible video files into the internal memory or Micro SD card (if inserted).

Yes, this eReader can function as a photo viewer. It supports JPG, GIF, and PNG image formats. To view photos: 1. Ensure your photos are copied to the device’s internal memory or inserted Micro SD card (up to 16GB). 2. From the Main Menu, select "Photo" <PIC>. 3. Use the navigation keys (↑ ↓ ← →) to browse images; press "M" (Confirm/Enter) to open and view a selected photo. 4. While viewing, you can zoom, pan, or rotate using on-screen controls or directional keys (as supported per firmware). For detailed operation, refer to the "Photo" section in the user manual. , ["Manual13_0"]

When using this eReader, you may encounter the following common issues and their solutions: 1. **Cannot power on**: - Charge the device fully via USB cable until the power indicator bar is full. - If still unresponsive, the device may be defective—contact manufacturer for support. 2. **Cannot charge**: - Check if the AC adapter or USB cable is defective; contact manufacturer for replacement. 3. **No sound or noise with earphones**: - Adjust volume (ensure it’s not at 0). - Try a different pair of earphones. - Use high-quality audio files—low-bitrate files may cause distortion. 4. **Cannot download files to the reader**: - Replace the USB cable if faulty. - Free up space—the internal memory (2GB) or Micro SD card (up to 16GB) may be full. - Windows 98 users may need to install a USB 2.0 driver (downloadable from the manufacturer’s website). 5. **Micro SD card not recognized**: - Power off the device, remove and reinsert the card carefully. - ⚠️ Always power off before inserting/removing the card—never reset the unit with the card inserted. 6. **Screen display issues (e.g., fading, slow response)**: - Avoid use outside the operating temperature range (32–120°F / 0–50°C), especially in cold outdoor winter conditions. 7. **General performance or file compatibility problems**: - This eReader supports TXT, EPUB, PDF, FB2, HTML (eBooks); MP3, WMA, WAV (audio); JPG, GIF, PNG (images); AVI, RMVB, MOV, MP4 (video). Unsupported or newly released formats may not play—contact tech support for assistance. For firmware updates, visit the manufacturer’s website. Always follow care guidelines: avoid moisture, drops, extreme temperatures, and never disassemble the device—contact authorized service for repairs.

To properly finish connecting your fax machine, follow these key steps: 1. Ensure the fax is placed near a telephone wall jack and a grounded AC power outlet. 2. Use only the provided standard modular RJ11C telephone cord to connect the fax to the wall jack — do not use party lines or coin service lines. 3. Plug the power cord fully into a grounded outlet (do not use extension cords or unapproved cables). 4. Verify the Ringer Equivalence Number (REN) of your fax and other connected devices does not exceed 5.0 on the same line. 5. After physical connections, power on the device and configure essential settings (e.g., date/time, station ID) via the control panel or Online User’s Guide. If the fax fails to register a dial tone or send/receive test faxes, check all cable connections, ensure no other devices are overloading the line, and consult the 'Quick Setup Guide' or contact Brother Customer Support. <PIC> , ["Manual15_0"]

To ensure your safety when using this fax, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. **Electrical Safety**: Use only the provided power cord and plug it into a grounded AC outlet; never use damaged, frayed, or unauthorized cords or extension cords. Unplug the device immediately if it becomes unusually hot, emits smoke or strong odors, or if liquid is spilled on it. 2. **Placement & Environment**: Place the fax on a flat, stable surface in a dry, well-ventilated area—away from water sources (e.g., sinks, bathtubs), direct sunlight, heat sources, flammable materials, and corrosive chemicals. Maintain operating conditions of 50–95°F (10–35°C) and 20–80% humidity with no condensation. 3. **Physical Safety**: Do not place objects on or near the fax (especially metal items or containers with liquid); avoid blocking ventilation slots or paper output areas; keep cables secured to prevent tripping. 4. **Usage Precautions**: Never use during thunderstorms; avoid touching telephone wires unless the line is unplugged; do not attempt self-repair—only authorized service personnel should open or service the unit due to risk of electric shock from high-voltage components. 5. **Emergency Access**: Ensure the power cord remains easily accessible so you can unplug it quickly in case of emergency.

To keep your fingers safe when using the fax, be careful not to put your fingers in the areas shown in the illustrations — these are typically paper feed paths, output trays, or internal mechanisms where moving parts or pinch points exist. Wait until pages have fully exited the product before picking them up, as reaching in prematurely may trap your fingers. Also, never insert your hand or any foreign objects into slots or openings (e.g., ink insertion slot, paper path), as this may cause injury. <PIC> , ["fax_01"]

When moving your fax machine, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. **Power & Cables**: Unplug the AC power cord and telephone line (RJ-11) before moving — never pull on the cord itself; grip the plug. Ensure no cables are pinched, stretched, or damaged during transport. 2. **Trays & Paper**: Remove all paper trays (especially if extended for large paper) to prevent tipping or injury — the weight of paper can cause trays to collapse or the unit to become unstable <PIC>. 3. **Physical Handling**: Carry the unit upright on a flat, stable surface; avoid tilting or laying it on its side, especially if ink cartridges are installed, to prevent ink leakage or internal damage <PIC>. 4. **Environment**: Place it in a new location with stable temperature (50–95°F / 10–35°C), humidity (20–80%), no direct sunlight, moisture, dust, or magnetic sources (e.g., speakers, microwaves) <PIC>. 5. **Safety Clearance**: Keep the area around the unit clear — do not block ventilation slots, power outlets, or the paper output path, and ensure no objects rest on top or obstruct moving parts <PIC>. 6. **Post-Move Check**: After setup, verify connections, run a test fax/print, and confirm date/time and station ID are correctly configured per the Online User’s Guide. , ["fax_01", "fax_04", "fax_06", "fax_07"]

Yes, caution or warning labels inside the fax are important. They provide critical safety information to prevent injury, electrical shock, fire, or equipment damage. According to the safety instructions: 'DO NOT remove or damage the caution or warning label inside the product.' Removing them may void your warranty, compromise safety compliance, and increase risk during operation or servicing. Please keep all labels intact and legible.

您好，关于您的问题：加拿大用户可以正常使用该传真产品。根据官方合规声明，本设备符合加拿大工业部（Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada）的免许可无线电标准（RSS-210、ICES-003 Class B），并明确标注为‘For Use in the USA or Canada Only’。同时，设备附带的《Industry Canada Compliance Statement》和《Canadian Department of Communications Statement》均确认其在加拿大合法销售与使用。此外，产品支持英语界面及加拿大本地电话线路规格（如RJ11接口、REN值适配等）。如需获取加拿大本地版用户手册、驱动程序或技术支持，您可访问品牌官网的加拿大站点（brother.ca）或联系Brother Canada客服中心。

To connect the regulator to the LP tank when using the grill, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the LP tank is properly secured onto or into the grill (e.g., placed into the bottom shelf hole with valve facing forward, and tightened with the cylinder screw). 2. Turn all grill control knobs to the OFF position. 3. Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop. 4. Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve (always use the supplied cap and strap when not in use). 5. Hold the regulator straight and align its nipple centrally into the LP tank valve opening. 6. Hand-tighten the coupling nut clockwise—do not use tools. Tighten until you feel resistance from the back-check feature in the valve, then continue with an additional half to three-quarters turn to complete the seal. ⚠️ Note: If connection cannot be completed, disconnect and repeat steps 5–6. If still unsuccessful, do not use the regulator—contact support. Also, always perform a leak check after connection and before lighting the grill (see Use & Care section).

To ensure your safety, perform a leak test on valves, hose, and regulator before each use or after any disconnection/reconnection. Follow these steps: 1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF. 2. Ensure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank. 3. Slowly open the LP tank valve (turn OPD hand wheel counterclockwise). If you hear a rushing sound, close the valve immediately — this indicates a major leak at the connection; contact Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME before proceeding. 4. Apply a soapy water solution (e.g., dish soap + water) to all connections: valve outlet, hose ends, regulator inlet/outlet, and hose surface. Watch for bubbles — any bubbling indicates a leak. <PIC> 5. If a leak is detected, STOP using the grill immediately. Close the LP tank valve fully (clockwise), ventilate the area, and do not operate any ignition sources. Call your LP gas supplier or fire department if the leak cannot be stopped. Never insert tools or foreign objects into valves — this can damage seals and cause leaks. Also, inspect hoses before each use for cuts, wear, or kinks; replace if damaged. Use only the valve/hose/regulator specified in the Parts List. , ["leak_test_diagram"]

When using indirect cooking on your grill, please pay attention to the following key points: 1. Always keep the grill lid closed during indirect cooking to maintain consistent heat circulation and ensure even, slow roasting. 2. Use only selected burners (not all) to generate gentle, circulating heat — avoid direct flame contact with food to minimize flare-ups, especially when cooking fatty cuts or poultry. 3. Adjust temperature settings upward in cold or windy conditions, as ambient weather may reduce internal grill temperature and extend cooking times. 4. Ensure proper ventilation — use the grill only in open, well-ventilated areas, never under covered structures or indoors. 5. Maintain safe clearances: position the grill at least 3 ft from walls/surfaces and 10 ft from flammable objects or ignition sources (e.g., water heater pilot lights). 6. Regularly inspect and clean burners if flame strength diminishes or lighting becomes difficult.

您好，根据提供的手册内容，未明确列出组装流程的完整步骤编号（如第1、2、3步），也未找到清晰定义的‘前三个组装步骤’的完整序列。手册中零散提及部分操作，例如： 1. 准备工具（需自备可调扳手、螺丝刀、7/16"组合扳手）； 2. 将烤炉头部抬起并小心放置到推车架上，对齐底部槽口与侧板立柱，并确保调节器软管垂入推车内，炉头朝向推车开口侧（该步骤需两人协作）； 3. 液化石油气（LP）钢瓶需单独购买，安装前须先充气并做泄漏检测，再放入底架孔位，阀口朝前，并用底架下方的气瓶螺丝固定。 由于手册未提供结构化、编号化的完整组装流程，建议您查阅随附纸质说明书中的‘Assembly’章节图示或联系官方客服（1-800-241-7548）获取带图解的分步指导。

Certainly! Here are key safety tips for using your grill safely: 1. **Location & Ventilation**: Use the grill only in a well-ventilated, open outdoor area — NEVER in enclosed spaces like garages, porches, carports, or under overhead structures. 2. **Clearance & Setup**: Place the grill at least 3 ft. from any wall or surface, and maintain a 10-ft. clearance from flammable objects (e.g., deck railings, overhanging branches, pilot lights, or electrical appliances). 3. **Children & Pets**: Keep children and pets away from the grill during operation and while it’s still hot. 4. **Gas Safety**: - Always check coupling nut tightness before opening the LP tank valve. - Turn off all control knobs AND the LP tank valve when not in use. - Never move the grill while operating or still hot. - Perform a leak test before first use and after any disconnection. - Do NOT attach to RV/motorhome LP systems. 5. **Fire Prevention**: - Keep the grill area clear of combustible materials. - Never use water on a grease fire — instead, turn off all knobs and the LP tank immediately. - Empty the grease tray after each use (only when fully cooled) to prevent flare-ups. - Clean burners and grates regularly to avoid grease buildup. 6. **Personal Protection**: Use long-handled utensils and heat-resistant oven mitts to avoid burns and splatters. 7. **Food Safety**: - Wash hands, utensils, and surfaces before/after handling raw meat. - Use a food thermometer: poultry (165°F), ground meats (160°F), steaks/roasts (145°F), pork (160°F). - Never partially grill and finish later — cook thoroughly in one session. 8. **Storage**: Store outdoors covered and dry; if storing indoors, disconnect and remove the LP tank and store it outdoors separately. For further assistance, contact the Grill Service Center at 1-800-241-7548.

Your jetski has three main identification numbers you should record for service, parts ordering, or theft recovery: 1. **Primary Identification (PRI-ID) number**: Stamped on a label inside the engine compartment <PIC>. 2. **Hull Identification Number (HIN)**: A unique 12-character identifier assigned to the hull — typically located on the starboard (right) side of the transom. 3. **Engine serial number**: Found on the engine block — refer to your owner’s manual for exact location. We recommend writing these numbers down and storing them separately from your jetski. You’ll need them when contacting an authorized dealer or filing insurance claims. For visual guidance on locating the PRI-ID label, please see the engine compartment diagram in your manual. , ["jetski_01"]

Your jetski has several important cruising limitations designed for safety and optimal performance: 1. **Occupancy & Load Limit**: This watercraft is designed for the operator and **one passenger only**—never exceed **2 persons** or the **maximum load limit of 160 kg (353 lb)**, which includes the total weight of operator, passenger, and all cargo. 2. **Operator Experience**: Do not carry a passenger until you have **considerable solo riding experience**, as handling with two people requires greater skill and familiarity with the watercraft’s behavior. 3. **Legal & Regulatory Limits**: As a legally classified powerboat, your jetski must comply with all local, state, and federal waterway rules—including speed limits in no-wake zones, right-of-way protocols, and restricted areas (e.g., near shorelines, wildlife habitats, swimmers, or docks). Always observe posted signage and maintain safe distances. For full details, refer to Section 'Cruising limitations' (page 11) and 'Rules of the Road' (page 17) in your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual.

Your jetski has several important cruising limitations designed for safety and optimal performance: 1. **Occupancy & Load Limit**: This watercraft is designed for the operator and **one passenger only**—never exceed **2 persons** or the **maximum load limit of 160 kg (353 lb)**, which includes the combined weight of operator, passenger, and all cargo. 2. **Operator Experience**: Do not operate with a passenger until you have considerable solo riding experience—passenger operation requires greater skill and familiarity with handling characteristics. 3. **Legal & Regulatory Limits**: As a legally classified powerboat, your jetski must comply with all local, state, and federal waterway rules—including speed limits, no-wake zones, right-of-way protocols, and restricted areas (e.g., near shorelines, wildlife habitats, swimmers, or fishing zones). 4. **Environmental & Responsible Use**: Cruising must respect others and nature—avoid excessive noise or wake near homes, beaches, nesting areas, or other water users; maintain safe distances and reduce speed where required. For full details, always consult your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual, especially Section 'Cruising limitations' (page 11), and complete a certified boating safety course as recommended by jetski and required in many states.

To safely operate your jetski, you must meet the following key requirements: 1. **Operator Eligibility**: Minimum age is 16 years old; minors require adult supervision. Check your state’s specific age and boating safety course requirements — a certified course is recommended and may be mandatory (contact USCG or NASBLA for local rules). 2. **Pre-Operation Preparation**: Read the Owner’s/Operator’s Manual, Riding Practice Guide, Riding Instruction card, and all warning labels; watch the Basic Orientation Video. Ensure every rider completes this training before operation. 3. **Safety Gear**: All riders must wear a U.S. Coast Guard–approved personal flotation device (PFD) rated for personal watercraft use. Additionally, wear protective clothing — such as a wetsuit bottom or thick, tightly woven, snug-fitting apparel (e.g., denim) — to prevent injury from forced water entry; swimwear or spandex-type fabrics are NOT sufficient. 4. **Operational Restrictions**: Do NOT operate at night (after sunset or before dawn) — the jetski lacks required lighting, increasing collision risk. 5. **Legal Compliance**: Jetskis are classified as powerboats — you must follow all applicable waterway 'Rules of the Road', including right-of-way rules (stand-on vs. give-way vessels), navigation rules for meeting, crossing, and overtaking situations, and local/state regulations enforced by the USCG or DMV.

Your jetski is a water-jet propelled watercraft, meaning it uses a jet pump directly connected to the engine—there is no neutral gear, and jet thrust will produce movement whenever the engine is running. Key characteristics include: 1) Propulsion via water jet (no propeller), 2) Integrated jet pump and engine, 3) No neutral position, 4) Critical safety features such as a cooling water pilot outlet <PIC>, bow eye for towing/mooring <PIC>, jet intake <PIC>, adjustable sponsons (YAS) for stability <PIC>, and dedicated storage compartments. It is legally classified as a powerboat and must comply with U.S. Coast Guard and international 'Rules of the Road' for navigation, right-of-way, and safe operation. Recommended onboard equipment includes a sound-signaling device, visual distress signals, a watch, and a towline. , ["drill0_04", "drill0_05", "drill0_06", "drill0_07"]

When operating your jetski and encountering other vessels, you must follow the 'Rules of the Road'—as your jetski is legally considered a powerboat. Key rules include: 1. **Right-of-Way Rules**: - The **stand-on vessel** (with right-of-way) must maintain course and speed—unless immediate collision avoidance requires action. - The **give-way vessel** (without right-of-way) must take *positive, timely, and visible* action—e.g., slow down, alter direction, and pass *behind* the stand-on vessel—not cross in front. - If collision appears unavoidable, *both vessels become give-way vessels* and must act to avoid it (General Prudential Rule). 2. **Three Encounter Scenarios**: - **Meeting head-on**: Both vessels should alter course to starboard (right) to pass port-to-port. - **Crossing**: The vessel approaching from the *starboard side* has right-of-way; you must yield if approaching from *port side*. - **Overtaking**: The vessel being overtaken is stand-on; you (overtaking) are always give-way—pass at safe distance and speed. 3. **General Safety Practices**: - Scan constantly for people, objects, and other watercraft. - Operate at safe speeds and maintain safe distances—never follow closely or spray others. - Avoid sharp turns or maneuvers that impair others’ ability to anticipate your path. - Always check behind before turning, and be extra vigilant—other boaters may not see you. For full, location-specific regulations, consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary, Department of Motor Vehicles, or NASBLA-approved resources like the free Online Boating Safety Course at jetski-motor.com.

To remove the seat: Pull the seat latch up, and then pull the seat off. To install the seat: Insert the projection on the front of the seat into the stay on the deck, and then push the rear of the seat down to lock it in place securely.
To open the hood of the jetski: Push the hood latch <PIC> down, then lift the hood upward. To close the hood: Gently push the hood down until it locks securely in place. , ["hood_latch_01"]

There are two main types of filler caps on the jet ski: the fuel tank filler cap and the oil tank filler cap. To remove either one, simply turn it counterclockwise. Make sure both caps are securely tightened before operating the watercraft. <PIC> <PIC> , ["EJU01012", "EJU10131"]

您好，关于喷射艇（jetski）上两种发动机开关的操作，请注意以下关键安全与操作规范： 1. **主开关钥匙（Main Switch Key）**： - 启动前确保 throttle 杠杆完全松开、水艇处于完全静止状态； - 启动时切勿在发动机运行中将主开关钥匙拨至 START 位置，否则可能损坏启动机构； - 单次启动尝试不得超过 5 秒，否则可能导致电池快速耗尽或启动电机损坏； - 停止发动机时：(1) 先将遥控操纵杆回至空档（Neutral）位置；(2) 再将主开关钥匙转至 OFF 位置。 2. **发动机急停开关（Engine Shut-off Switch）及熄火绳（Lanyard）**： - 清理进水口或叶轮区域前，**必须先关闭发动机，并拔下熄火绳插头**，以防接触高速旋转部件造成严重伤害或死亡； - 熄火绳应始终连接至驾驶员身上，一旦意外落水可自动切断发动机； - 急停开关通常为红色按钮或拉绳式，用于紧急情况下立即停机。 ⚠️ 重要警告： - 发动机停止后，船舶将**完全丧失转向控制**，极易发生碰撞； - 高速运行后请勿立即停机，建议先怠速或低速运转数分钟以冷却发动机； - 切勿在发动机运行时操作进水口/喷嘴区域，严禁将任何物体插入喷射口。 如需进一步确认开关位置或图示说明，建议查阅随附说明书第 X 页的 'Switches' 章节（参考文档 5 & 6）。

Jet ski 上通常有两类操纵杆：1. **遥控操纵杆（Remote control levers）**：用于控制前进/倒车及速度。向前推为前进，向后拉为倒车；推得越远，喷射推力越大，船速越快（全部推至最前时，转向门完全上抬，所有喷射推力向后，实现全速前进）<PIC>；操作前请确保转向门未被触碰，以防夹伤。2. **倾角调节杆（Tilt lever）**：位于方向盘下方，用于调节方向盘俯仰角度，共5档可调。操作方法：下压杠杆 → 上下移动方向盘至所需位置 → 松开杠杆使其自动锁止 → 确认方向盘已牢固锁定且杠杆复位。⚠️ 警告：行驶中严禁触碰倾角调节杆，否则方向盘可能突然移位，导致事故。<PIC> , ["drill0_04", "drill0_06"]

When using the Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector on your jetski, you should obtain and follow this key information: 1. **Location**: The QSTS selector $\textcircled{1}$ is located at the left handlebar grip. 2. **Function**: It adjusts the trim angle by changing the vertical angle of the jet thrust nozzle — affecting bow-up or bow-down attitude for optimal handling and performance. 3. **Positions**: There are 5 trim positions: - 2 bow-down positions ($\circledast$, $\circleddash$), - Neutral ($\Lsh$), - 2 bow-up positions ($\copyright$, $\circledcirc$). 4. **Safe Operation Procedure**: - Reduce engine speed to **3,000 r/min or less** before adjusting. - Squeeze the shift lock lever $\circledcirc$, then turn the QSTS selector to your desired position. - Release the shift lock lever to lock it in place. 5. **Critical CAUTION**: - ❗ **Never turn the QSTS selector while operating at full throttle** — doing so may cause damage to the system. 6. **Usage Guidance**: - The neutral (“N”) position provides good all-around performance for most conditions. - Use bow-down for improved acceleration and planing; bow-up for higher top speed and reduced spray at high speeds. Always refer to your Owner’s/Operator’s Manual and observe all warning labels for safe, reliable operation.

The fuel meter shows the amount of fuel remaining in the tank using eight segments — it's for convenient fuel level checking while riding. Note that the indication may vary slightly depending on operating conditions, so use it as a general reference. The hour meter tracks the total hours the engine has been running since the watercraft was new, helping you follow the recommended maintenance schedule. Both are part of the multifunction information center and appear on the trip screen along with other operational data.

您好，关于喷射艇（Jetski）燃油滤清器和燃油箱的检查，根据官方手册说明： 1. **燃油滤清器检查与更换**： - ⚠️ **严禁自行拆卸或更换燃油滤清器**。错误安装可能导致汽油泄漏，引发火灾或爆炸。 - 燃油滤清器位于发动机左前方（参考图66），更换周期为每500小时或每年一次（以先到者为准，恶劣环境需更频繁）。 - 如确需更换，请务必由雅马哈授权经销商操作，确保安全与合规。 2. **燃油箱检查**： - 检查燃油箱（标注为 $\textcircled{1}$）是否存在泄漏或进水现象。 - 若发现燃油系统中有水分，或燃油箱需清洁，请立即联系雅马哈授权经销商进行专业服务。 - 同时建议定期清洁燃油加注口盖周围（参考文档2），防止污物进入油路。 3. **附加安全提醒**： - 检查燃油管路、接头及夹箍是否有破损、松动或渗漏迹象（至少每年一次，或怀疑异常时）；如发现漏油，**切勿启动或运行机器**，须立即检修。 - 所有燃油相关操作均须在发动机完全冷却、钥匙拔出、熄火拉绳断开的状态下进行，严防火灾风险。 如需进一步协助（如查找附近授权经销商或预约服务），欢迎随时告知，我们将全力支持。

To adjust the adjustable sponsons on your jetski, follow these steps: 1. Remove the bolts $\textcircled{1}$ on both sponsons. 2. Remove both sponsons and reinstall them in your desired position — ensure both are installed to the same level. 3. Tighten the bolts on both sponsons to the correct torque (refer to your owner's manual for exact specification; typical value is 49 Nm if applicable per similar adjustment systems). ⚠️ Important: Always perform this adjustment with the watercraft unloaded (no rider or cargo), and confirm symmetrical positioning on both sides for balanced handling.

To safely clean the jet intake and impeller of your jetski, follow these critical steps: 1. **Shut down completely**: Turn off the engine, remove the main switch key, and disconnect the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the switch. Wait for all rotating parts to stop. ⚠️ WARNING: Contact with rotating impeller parts can cause severe injury or death. 2. **Beach the watercraft** — do not attempt cleaning while in water. 3. **Turn the watercraft onto its port (left) side**, placing a clean cloth or carpet underneath to prevent scratches; support the bow to avoid handlebar damage. 4. **Remove weeds/debris** from around the drive shaft, impeller, pump housing, and jet thrust nozzle using appropriate non-metallic tools. Avoid sharp objects that could damage components. 5. If debris is stubborn or impeller blades appear bent/damaged, **do not force removal** — contact an authorized Yamaha dealer for service. Note: Clogged intakes cause cavitation (loss of thrust despite rising RPM), leading to overheating and potential engine seizure.

您好，关于您的问题：'How to start my jetski in different situations?'（在不同情况下如何启动我的水上摩托？），目前提供的参考知识中未包含具体的启动步骤、不同情境（如冷车启动、水中重启、带乘客启动等）的操作说明或相关图文指引。为确保安全与正确操作，建议您：1. 查阅随车附带的《Owner's/Operator's Manual》（用户/操作手册）及《Riding Practice Guide》（骑行练习指南）；2. 观看配套的 Basic Orientation Video（基础导向视频）；3. 启动前务必确认周围无船只、障碍物或游泳者（参考警告信息）；4. 确保发动机熄火绳（engine shut-off cord）已正确连接至手腕并插入开关。如需进一步技术指导，可访问 jetski-motor.com 网站获取官方在线资源或联系授权服务中心。

Boarding a jet ski with a passenger in deep water requires caution and proper sequence to ensure safety. Here are the basic steps: 1. Climb on board first, sit astride the seat, and attach the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) securely to your wrist, then connect the clip to the engine shut-off switch. 2. Have the passenger move to the rear of the watercraft — **engines must be OFF** to avoid jet thrust or carbon monoxide hazards. 3. The passenger pulls up onto the swim platform (if equipped), kneels, then carefully crawls onto the seat <PIC>. 4. Both operator and passenger should balance the watercraft during boarding. 5. Once seated, the passenger must place both feet firmly on the floor of the footwell and hold on securely to the operator. 6. Only then may you start the engine, check all directions, and accelerate gradually to planing speed. ⚠️ WARNING: Never apply throttle until the passenger is fully seated and holding on — severe internal injuries can result from proximity to the jet thrust nozzle. , ["Manual40_26"]

If it is difficult to balance the jetski at a standstill, follow these steps: 1. The passenger must steady the watercraft while the operator boards; the operator pulls up onto the platform into a seated position and then balances the craft. 2. Both operator and passenger should actively help maintain balance during boarding. 3. Use the onboard boarding step (if equipped) to assist with stable entry — it can be lowered for easier boarding and automatically retracts afterward <PIC>. 4. Practice boarding and balancing repeatedly until confident in maintaining stability throughout the process. , ["Manual40_13"]

The maximum load of the jetski is 160 kg (353 lb), which includes the total weight of the operator, passenger, and any cargo. This watercraft is designed for the operator and one passenger only — never exceed 2 persons or the 160 kg limit.

The base station of a landline cordless phone (e.g., model XL495) serves as the central hub that connects to the telephone line and power supply, enabling communication with registered handsets. It typically features: a telephone socket (to connect to the wall phone line), a power socket (to connect to AC power), and a charging cradle for the handset. The base station manages call routing, signal transmission to handsets, and may support additional functions like an integrated answering machine (as referenced in Section 12 of the manual). For proper setup, both the line cord and power cord must be connected — one end to the base station’s respective sockets, the other to the wall telephone jack and wall power outlet. Signal strength between the base and handset is critical; if no signal bars appear, the handset may need re-registration by placing it on the base until the signal bar displays.

To connect the base station of a landline phone, follow these steps: 1. Connect one end of the telephone line cord to the telephone socket at the bottom of the base station, and the other end to the telephone wall socket. 2. Connect the supplied power adapter: plug one end into the DC input jack at the bottom of the base station, and the other end into a wall power socket. 3. Ensure the power supply voltage matches the value printed on the bottom or underside of the base station (check the type plate). 4. If you use DSL internet service on the same telephone line, install a DSL filter between the telephone line cord and the wall socket to prevent interference with caller ID or audio quality. 5. Plug the power adapter into mains — your base station is now powered and ready for use.

To install the handset of a landline telephone, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the batteries are pre-installed in the handset; pull off the battery tape from the battery door before charging. 2. Connect one end of the line cord to the telephone socket at the bottom of the base station, and the other end to the wall telephone socket. In some countries, you may need to connect a line adapter to the line cord first, then plug it into the wall socket. 3. Plug the base station’s power adapter into a wall power socket (Step 4: Plug into mains). 4. For multi-handset systems, additional handsets must be registered: press and hold the registration button on the base station to enter registration mode, then press the 'Find Handset' button on the new handset. Always read the safety instructions before installation.

To check the battery level of the landline handset: 1. Press the battery level display button on the right-hand side of the handset or case. 2. Alternatively, place the handset on the charger or in the travel case — the battery indication will show the current level. 3. If the handset is low on battery, you may hear warning tones during a call. Also, check the LED indicator by navigating to [Menu] > [Phone setup] > [Charge status].

If your landline handset is in 'searching' status, try the following steps: 1. Ensure the base station has power supply. 2. Register the handset to the base station — press and hold the registration button (or follow [Menu] > [Services] > [Register your Handset]). 3. Move the handset closer to the base station — weak signal or distance can cause searching behavior. 4. Check if ECO+ mode is activated; if so, turn it off to improve connection stability and range. 5. If the issue persists, disconnect power from both handset and base station for 1 minute, then reconnect and retry.

您好，关于座机基站（base station）LED指示灯的不同状态含义，参考文档提到存在一张表格说明各LED行为对应当前设备状态，但当前知识库中未提供该表格的具体内容（例如：常亮、闪烁、颜色变化等分别代表待机、通话中、信号弱、充电中等）。因此，暂无法为您详细解释每种LED行为的具体含义。建议您查阅产品说明书中的 'LED Indicator Behavior' 章节，或提供具体LED现象（如：哪个灯、什么颜色、如何闪烁），我将尽力协助判断。

The LED indicator behavior on the base station reflects its current status. Common patterns include: both LEDs blinking simultaneously <PIC>, both LEDs blinking alternatively <PIC>, and status-specific colors — green indicating ON/ready state and red indicating OFF/error state <PIC>. For precise interpretation, refer to the full LED status table in your user manual, as exact meanings (e.g., charging, pairing, low signal) depend on your specific model. If you're experiencing unexpected LED behavior, ensure the base station is powered, properly connected to the phone line, and within range of the handset. , ["drill0_04", "drill0_05", "drill0_06"]

To lower the roll bar of the lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Remove the hairpin cotters and then remove the 2 pins (Figure 9). 2. Lower the roll bar to the down position (Figure 8). Note: There are 2 down positions — refer to Figure 8 for correct alignment. <PIC> <PIC> , ["Figure_9", "Figure_8"]

To adjust the rear-shock assemblies on a lawn mower with suspension system only: 1. Locate the slotted mounting positions for the rear-shock assemblies — these slots include detent positions for reference, but you may position the assemblies anywhere within the slot (not limited to detents). 2. Move the shock assemblies to your preferred position to achieve either a softer or firmer ride, as illustrated in Figure 13. 3. Ensure equal spring preload on both left and right sides — mismatched preload may cause handling imbalance <PIC>. 4. Note: This adjustment changes the total suspension spring rate and damping characteristics. Per safety guidance, this adjustment should be performed by an authorized dealer <PIC>. , ["drill0_12", "drill0_13"]

For lawn mowers with an electric deck lift, adjust the height of cut as follows: 1. Push up on the deck-lift switch (Figure 31) to raise the deck. 2. Push down on the deck-lift switch (Figure 31) to set and lower the deck to the desired cutting height. The electric deck lift allows smooth, tool-free height adjustment — no manual pin relocation is required for this type of system. Note: The clevis pin method described in other sections applies only to models with a mechanical deck-lift pedal, not electric lift systems. <PIC> , ["fig31"]

The term 'load a lawn mower' typically refers to loading it onto a trailer or transport vehicle—not loading fuel, oil, or grass clippings. Based on the reference documentation, here's the correct procedure: 1. **Use only one ramp**, wide enough for the mower (do not use dual ramps). 2. Ensure the ramp slope is **less than 15°**. 3. **Back the mower up the ramp** when loading. 4. When unloading, **drive forward off the ramp**. 5. Before loading, shut off the engine, remove the key, engage the parking brake, and ensure all moving parts have stopped. 6. Close the fuel-shutoff valve (Figure 36) for safety during transport. ⚠️ Important safety warnings: - Never operate the mower near drop-offs or on slopes >15°. - Keep hands/feet away from under the deck; never attempt adjustments or cleaning while blades could rotate. - Always follow the Operator’s Manual and ensure you are trained before operating. For visual guidance, refer to ramp-loading instructions in the manual (no specific figure number provided for loading, but ramp safety is emphasized in Document 4).

To unload the lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Lower the ramp, ensuring the angle between the ramp and the ground does not exceed 15 degrees <PIC>. 2. Drive the machine forward down the ramp <PIC>. Always ensure the engine is off, the key is removed, and the parking brake is engaged before unloading. For safety, never allow untrained personnel to handle the machine during unloading. , ["drill0_39", "drill0_40"]

To change the engine oil of a lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Start the engine and let it run for 5 minutes to warm the oil for better drainage. 2. Park the machine on a level surface with the drain side slightly lower to ensure complete oil drainage. 3. Disengage the blade-control switch (PTO) and engage the parking brake. 4. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop. 5. Drain the oil from the engine (refer to Figure 49 — not provided in available images). Note: Dispose of used oil at a recycling center. For some models, you may also need to close the fuel-shutoff valve and ensure the machine is cool before servicing <PIC>. Recommended oil: 10W-40 for most conditions. , ["Manual18_42"]

To remove the air filters from a lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake. 2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop. 3. Release the latches on the air cleaner and pull the air-inlet cover off the air-cleaner body <PIC>. 4. Remove the primary filter and inner filter (if replacement is intended) — note: never clean the inner filter; if it’s dirty, replace both the primary and inner filters. Refer to Figure 57 for filter locations and assembly details. , ["fig57"]

To replace the mower belt of a lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake. 2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position. 3. Lower the mower deck to the 76 mm (3 inches) height of cut. 4. Remove the belt covers (Figure 73). 5. Use a 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole in the idler arm to remove tension on the idler spring (Figure 74). 6. Remove the belt from the mower-deck pulleys and the clutch pulley. 7. Remove the belt guide on the spring-loaded idler arm (Figure 74). 8. Remove the existing belt. 9. Install the new belt around the mower pulleys and the clutch pulley under the engine (Figure 74). , ["drill0_73", "drill0_74"]

To set up control of an over-the-range microwave (e.g., models HMV9302–HMV9307), follow these key steps based on the control panel and setup features: 1. **Initial Setup & Clock**: Press the **Clock** pad → enter current time using number pads → press **Start** to save. 2. **Control Set-Up (Default Preferences)**: Press **Control Set-Up** to customize settings such as: - Sound On/Off - Clock format (12h/24h) - Display scroll speed - Defrost weight unit (Lbs or Kg) 3. **Child Lock Activation**: To prevent accidental use, press **Stop/Clear**, then press and hold the **0** pad for >4 seconds until "LOCKED" appears and two beeps sound. To unlock, repeat the same step. 4. **Vent Fan Control**: Press **Vent HI/LO/Off** to cycle through High → Low → Off. Note: If internal temperature rises too high, the fan may auto-activate at Low and run up to 1 hour — it cannot be manually turned off during this safety mode. 5. **Light Control**: Press **Light HI/LO/Off** to toggle cooktop light brightness; press **Light Timer** to schedule automatic low-light activation/deactivation (e.g., on at 2:00 AM, off at 7:00 AM). 6. **Turntable Control**: Press **T/Table On/Off** to enable/disable rotation (note: unavailable during Sensor Cook or Defrost modes). 7. **Quick Cooking & Sensors**: Use dedicated one-touch pads (**Popcorn**, **Potato**, **Pizza**, **Vegetable**, **Cook**, **Reheat**) — the oven’s humidity sensor automatically detects food moisture and adjusts time/power. 8. **Manual Cooking**: Press **Power Level**, select desired level (e.g., 100%, 50%), then enter time with number pads → press **Start**. For full guidance, refer to pages 10–13 (Control Set-Up, Clock, Power Level) and page 9 (Control Panel layout) in the manual. <PIC> , ["Manual36_0"]

To set the Light Timer on your over-the-range microwave: 1. First, ensure the clock is set correctly (see 'Setting the Clock' in the manual). 2. Press the MENU button, then use △/▽ to navigate to the menu. 3. Press ▶ and then △/▽ to select 'Light Timer'. 4. Press ▶, then use △/▽ to set the desired 'On' time (e.g., 2:00 AM). 5. Press ▶ again, then use △/▽ to set the desired 'Off' time (e.g., 7:00 AM). 6. Press ▶ and use △/▽ to select 'On' to activate the timer. 7. Press MENU repeatedly to exit. Note: The light will automatically turn on at the low setting daily at the set 'On' time and turn off at the 'Off' time. To cancel the Light Timer while in operation, touch 'Light HI/LO/Off' pad.

To set your favorite recipe on this over-the-range microwave: 1. First, program your desired cooking settings (e.g., time, power level) as usual. 2. Once the settings are displayed, press the 'Favorite Recipe' button — this saves the current cooking instruction into memory. 3. To recall and start it later, simply press 'Favorite Recipe' again, then press 'Start'. Only one recipe can be stored at a time; saving a new one overwrites the previous one. Note: This feature is designed for quick one-touch re-cooking of your most-used setting.

To reheat food in your over-the-range microwave, you can use the dedicated **Reheat (Sensor)** function — a convenient preset feature that automatically detects moisture and adjusts time and power level for optimal results. It offers three categories: **Casserole**, **Dinner Plate**, and **Soup/Sauce**. Simply select the appropriate category, place food in a microwave-safe dish, close the door, and press Start. For best results: • Cover food with a vented lid or microwave-safe plastic wrap to retain moisture (uncover for crispness if desired). • Stir or rotate food halfway through reheating (if not using Sensor Reheat) to ensure even heating. • Let food stand for 1–2 minutes after reheating to allow heat to distribute evenly and complete cooking internally. • Avoid overheating — start with shorter times and add more as needed. Note: Do not reheat sealed containers, whole eggs, or foods in narrow-necked bottles — they may explode. Always use microwave-safe cookware.

To use the Auto Defrost feature on your over-the-range microwave: 1. Ensure food is raw and frozen at 0°F (directly from the freezer); avoid using it for refrigerated or partially thawed items. 2. Enter the **net weight** of the food in pounds and tenths of a pound (e.g., 1.3 lb), excluding packaging or containers — refer to the Weight Conversion Chart if needed. 3. Select the appropriate Auto Defrost setting (Meat, Poultry, or Fish) — the oven has three preset sequences. 4. During defrosting, the microwave will beep and pause; open the door to turn, separate, or rearrange food, and remove already-thawed portions. Return frozen pieces and press START to resume. 5. For best results: elevate food on a microwave-safe roasting rack; shield thin or fatty areas (e.g., chicken wings, fish tails) with small pieces of aluminum foil — ensuring foil does not touch oven walls; allow standing time after defrosting, as it’s built into the cycle. Note: Defrost times are approximate guidelines — always check food temperature and texture before cooking.

To clean the grease filter of an over-the-range microwave: 1. Remove the filter by sliding it to the side, pulling it downward, and pushing it to the other side until it drops out. 2. Wash the filter in the dishwasher **or** soak it in hot water with a mild detergent, then rinse thoroughly and shake dry. Do not use ammonia or abrasive cleaners — these may damage the aluminum filter (which may darken over time). Clean the filter at least once a month, and more frequently if used heavily. Also, avoid using lye-based oven cleaners, as they are corrosive and can harm the filter.

To replace the charcoal filter of your over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Unplug the oven or turn off power at the main power supply. 2. Remove the two vent grill mounting screws (the two middle screws). 3. Tip the grill forward, then lift it out. 4. Remove the old charcoal filter. 5. Slide a new charcoal filter into place — it should rest at the angle shown <PIC>. Note: The charcoal filter cannot be cleaned and must be replaced every 6 to 12 months (more often if needed). Never operate the oven hood without the filter in place. , ["Manual31_25"]

To replace the oven light of an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps carefully: 1. Unplug the microwave or turn off power at the main power supply. (CAUTION: To avoid electric shock or injury, always disconnect power first.) 2. Remove the vent cover mounting screws (typically the two middle screws). 3. Tip the vent cover forward, then lift it out to remove. 4. Remove the bulb cover and mounting screws. 5. Pull out the old bulb — avoid touching the halogen bulb with bare fingers, as skin oils can cause premature failure. 6. Replace the bulb with a 30 or 40 watt appliance bulb (halogen type, rated for 120V; do not use higher wattage). 7. Reinstall the bulb holder (if removed), then replace the bulb cover and mounting screws. 8. Slide the bottom of the vent cover into place, push the top until it snaps in, and reinstall the mounting screws. 9. Restore power at the main supply and test the light. # CAUTION: Wear gloves when handling the bulb and ensure the unit is fully cooled before servicing.

Setting the PCI Express 3.0 x16 slots involves both physical installation and BIOS configuration: 1. **Physical Installation**: Insert the PCIe 3.0 x16 graphics or expansion card fully into the designated slot (e.g., PCIe 3.0 x16_1, gray-colored slot for best performance in single-GPU mode), secure it with the bracket screw, and ensure adequate power supply and chassis cooling (e.g., connect chassis fans to CHA_FAN1/2/3). Unplug the power cord before installation. 2. **BIOS Configuration**: After booting, enter BIOS Setup (Chapter 2) and configure under relevant menus: - Under **2.6.4 PCH-IO Configuration** → **PCI Express Configuration**, set **PCIe Speed** to [Auto] (default) or manually select [Gen3] to enforce PCIe 3.0 mode. - Under **2.6.3 System Agent (SA) Configuration** → **PEG Port Configuration**, adjust **PCIEx16_1 / PCIEx16_2 Link Speed** to [Gen3] if needed. - Ensure **Primary Display** (under Graphics Configuration) is set to [PCIE] when using a discrete GPU. - Optional power management: Enable **PCI Express Native Power Management** and configure **PEG ASPM** as needed (e.g., [Auto] or [ASPM L0sL1]).

您好，关于主板跳线（jumpers）设置： 1. 跳线通常用于配置硬件功能，例如清除RTC RAM（实时时钟内存）——对应2针CLRTC跳线头，短接该跳线可清除CMOS中的日期、时间及系统设置参数（如密码）； 2. 设置前请务必断开电源（拔掉电源线），并做好防静电措施（如佩戴防静电手环或触摸接地金属）； 3. 跳线帽（jumper cap）应准确套在指定针脚上，切勿误插至风扇接口（如CPU_FAN、CHA_FAN等），这些是供电接口，非跳线，强行加帽可能导致损坏； 4. 具体跳线位置与功能请严格参照主板说明书中的‘Motherboard Layout’图示及‘Jumpers’章节（如CLRTC标识位置）。 由于不同主板型号跳线定义差异较大，建议您提供具体主板型号，以便获取精准指导。

Setting the rear panel connectors of a motherboard is not a user-configurable process — they are fixed, physical I/O ports (e.g., USB, audio, HDMI, Ethernet) integrated into the motherboard and aligned with the chassis rear I/O shield during installation. To properly use them: 1. Ensure the motherboard is correctly oriented in the chassis: the edge with external ports must face the rear of the chassis <PIC>. 2. Install the included I/O shield into the chassis’s rear slot before mounting the motherboard. 3. Secure the motherboard using standoffs and screws at designated screw holes — do not over-tighten. 4. Connect internal headers (e.g., front panel USB/audio, system panel) to their corresponding motherboard connectors as labeled in the manual. Note: Rear panel functionality (e.g., audio codec mode) may require BIOS configuration — for example, front panel audio type ([HD] vs [AC97]) is set in BIOS under 'Onboard Devices Configuration'. Always power off and unplug the system before handling components to prevent damage. , ["Manual02_6"]

You can control certain onboard LEDs (e.g., SupremeFX LED or Model Name LED) via BIOS or the Ai Suite 3 LED Control app. In BIOS, navigate to Advanced > Onboard Devices Configuration > SupremeFX LED Lighting or Model Name LED Lighting to turn them off or change lighting modes. Note: The Standby Power LED (SB_PWR) is hardware-controlled and cannot be configured—it automatically lights up when the system has standby power (ON, sleep, or soft-off mode).

You can update the BIOS of a motherboard using one of the following officially supported methods: 1. **Via EZ Flash 3 (Recommended, in BIOS Setup)**: - Boot into BIOS Setup by pressing <Delete> or <F2> during POST. - Press <F7> to enter Advanced Mode. - Go to the Tool menu → select "EZ Flash 3 Utility" and press <Enter>. - Choose update source: • **By USB**: Insert a FAT32/16-formatted USB drive (single partition only) containing the latest BIOS file (e.g., `Z17PG.CAP`). Press <Tab> to switch to Drive → select the USB → press <Tab> again → navigate to the BIOS file in Folder Info → press <Enter> to start update. • **By Internet**: Select "by Internet", choose a network connection method, and follow onscreen prompts (requires active Internet connection). - After completion, reboot the system. 2. **Via CrashFree BIOS 3 (Auto-recovery mode)**: - Used when BIOS is corrupted or fails to boot. - Rename the latest BIOS file to `Z17PG.CAP`, then save it on a USB flash drive or burn it to the Support DVD. - Power on the system with the device inserted — the utility auto-detects and launches EZ Flash 3. - After recovery, enter BIOS Setup and press <F5> to load Optimized Defaults for stability. 3. **Via BIOS Updater (DOS environment)**: - Prepare a FAT32/16 USB drive with the latest BIOS file and BIOS Updater (NTFS not supported in FreeDOS). - Boot from USB or Support DVD (press <F8> at startup → select boot device). - Run BIOS Updater, navigate to the BIOS file (use <Tab>, <Up/Down>), confirm with "Yes", then restart after completion. 4. **Via EZ Update (Windows-based, optional)**: - Install AI Suite 3, launch EZ Update from its main menu. - Enables automatic updates of BIOS, drivers, and software (requires Internet connection). ⚠️ **Critical Warnings**: - DO NOT power off, reset, or interrupt power during any BIOS update — this may cause boot failure. - Always download the *latest* BIOS file from the official motherboard support page. - After updating, load Optimized Defaults (<F5>) to ensure compatibility and stability. - Backup original BIOS is not supported due to security regulations.

To create RAID on your motherboard, follow these steps: 1. Enter BIOS and press <F11> or click to open the EZ Tuning Wizard. 2. Click 'RAID' then click 'Yes' to enable RAID mode — ensure SATA Mode in BIOS is set to [RAID] (not default [AHCI]) and all target HDDs are connected to Intel SATA connectors. 3. Confirm no existing RAID volumes exist on the drives. 4. Select your desired RAID type: for data redundancy, choose 'Easy Backup (RAID1)' or 'Easy Backup (RAID10)'; for performance, choose 'Super Speed'. 5. Click 'Yes' to confirm and proceed. 6. After setup completes, click 'Yes' to exit and 'OK' to reset the system. ⚠️ Before starting, consult your motherboard’s manual (included in the support DVD) for model-specific guidance and compatibility.

To install an operating system using SATA ODD and USB devices on this motherboard (especially for OS 7 on 100-series platforms), follow Method 1 from the documentation: 1. Prepare the OS installation source: Insert the OS 7 installation DVD into a **USB-connected optical disc drive (USB ODD)**, OR copy all files from the OS 7 DVD onto a **USB storage device** (≥8 GB, preferably formatted beforehand). 2. Connect the USB ODD or USB storage device to your motherboard. 3. Insert the **motherboard Support DVD** into an onboard **SATA ODD**. 4. Power on the system and press **F8 during POST** to enter the boot device selection screen. 5. Select the USB device (ODD or storage) as the boot device. 6. During early OS setup, the system will automatically load USB 3.0 drivers from the Support DVD in the SATA ODD — enabling USB keyboard/mouse functionality. 7. Once the 'Setup is starting...' screen appears, the USB 3.0 drivers have loaded successfully. 8. Proceed with the OS installation using onscreen instructions. Note: This method is specifically validated for Windows 7 on 100-series chipsets requiring preloaded USB 3.0 drivers. For other OS versions (e.g., Windows 8.1/10), ensure UEFI boot compatibility, correct SATA mode (AHCI/RAID), and up-to-date firmware/drivers.

To secure the motherboard to the chassis: 1. Ensure the motherboard is correctly oriented — the edge with external I/O ports must face the rear of the chassis <PIC>. 2. Align the motherboard’s screw holes with the corresponding standoffs in the chassis. 3. Insert and tighten **nine screws** into the holes marked by circles in the manual — do **not over-tighten**, as this may damage the motherboard. Always unplug the power cord and follow ESD precautions before handling. , ["Manual14_5"]

To prepare for CPU installation on the motherboard, follow these essential steps: 1. Unplug all power cables from the wall socket and ensure the ATX power supply is off to prevent electrical damage or injury. 2. Ground yourself using an antistatic wrist strap or by touching a grounded metal object (e.g., power supply case) to avoid electrostatic discharge (ESD). 3. Verify that your CPU is compatible with the LGA1151 socket — do NOT install CPUs designed for LGA1150/1155/1156 sockets. 4. Check that the PnP cap is intact and properly seated on the socket, and confirm no socket contacts are bent or damaged; retain the cap for warranty/RMA purposes. 5. Handle the CPU only by its edges — never touch the gold contacts or ICs. 6. Use only the dedicated CPU Installation Tool: hold it firmly on both sides, and ensure the CPU is correctly oriented and firmly clicked into place before final seating. 7. If installing the heatsink/fan later, prepare Thermal Interface Material (TIM) for application to both CPU and heatsink base.

To determine the system memory specifications and configuration of a motherboard, you can refer to the following key points from the documentation: 1. **Memory Type & Slots**: This motherboard supports four DDR4 DIMM sockets (Double Data Rate 4). DDR4 modules are physically incompatible with DDR, DDR2, or DDR3 slots due to different notches — do not mix them. 2. **Dual-Channel Operation**: You may install different memory sizes in Channel A and Channel B. The system uses the smaller channel’s size for dual-channel mode; any excess capacity from the larger channel runs in single-channel mode. 3. **Voltage & Compatibility**: DIMM voltage should be below 1.65V to protect the CPU. For best stability and compatibility, use memory modules with identical CAS Latency, same version/data code (D/C), and preferably from the same vendor — consult the motherboard’s Qualified Vendors List (QVL) for tested DDR4 3400 MHz (O.C.) modules. 4. **Capacity & OS Limitations**: On 32-bit OS, even with ≥4GB installed, usable memory is typically ~3GB or less due to address space limitations. To fully utilize 4GB or more, install a 64-bit OS and enable 'Memory Remap' in BIOS (under Memory Configuration). 5. **Speed & SPD**: Default memory frequency is determined by the module’s Serial Presence Detect (SPD) data. Overclocked modules may run below rated speed by default; manually adjust frequency via BIOS > Ai Tweaker menu (Section 2.5). 6. **Cooling & Load**: For full 4-DIMM load or overclocking, ensure adequate memory cooling for system stability. You can also view real-time memory size and configuration in BIOS under 'System Information' (e.g., 'Memory Size') or via OS tools like Windows Task Manager > Performance > Memory.

The Serial port connector (10-1 pin COM) on the motherboard is an internal header designed to support a serial (COM) port interface. It requires a separate serial port module (purchased separately), which connects to this 10-1 pin header via a cable; the module is then installed into a slot opening at the back of the system chassis. This connector is not pre-populated — it enables legacy serial communication only when the optional COM module is installed and the serial port is enabled in BIOS under 'Serial Port Configuration' (default I/O address: 3F8h, IRQ: 4).

The TPM connector (14-1 pin TPM) is a dedicated header on the motherboard designed to support a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) system. It enables secure storage of cryptographic keys, digital certificates, passwords, and sensitive data. A TPM enhances network security, protects digital identities, and ensures platform integrity. This connector is distinct from fan, power, or serial connectors — it is specifically for attaching a compatible TPM module (sold separately in some cases). Ensure correct orientation when installing the TPM module, as misalignment may prevent proper functionality.

The Thermal Sensor connector (2-pin T_SENSOR) is a dedicated 2-pin header on the motherboard designed to connect an external thermistor cable. It enables monitoring of temperature from custom or third-party thermal sensors placed on critical components or inside the chassis. According to the documentation, this sensor input can be selected as a temperature source for fan control — for example, in the Q-Fan Configuration menu under 'Chassis Fan 1/2/3 Q-Fan Source' or 'Extension Fan 1/2/3 Q-Fan Source', where '[T_Sensor]' is explicitly listed as a configurable option. This allows fans to dynamically adjust speed based on readings from the connected thermistor, enhancing thermal management flexibility beyond built-in sensors (e.g., CPU, Motherboard, VRM, PCH).

To set Quick Release (QR) for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: Press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position. This stops cooking quickly and is ideal for delicate foods like vegetables or seafood. ⚠️ Important safety notes: Never lean over or place hands/face near the steam release valve during venting; keep skin away from escaping steam. Always wait for the float valve to drop before opening the lid. For high-starch or expanding foods (e.g., beans, rice, oatmeal), Natural Release (NR) is recommended instead of Quick Release unless specified in the recipe.

To set the pressure cooking lid for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the float valve is properly installed — insert the narrow end into the float valve hole on the top of the lid <PIC>, then flip the lid over and firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve <PIC>. 2. Confirm the anti-block shield is in place to prevent food particles from blocking the steam release pipe <PIC>. 3. Check that the sealing ring is correctly seated, undamaged, and not stretched — replace it every 12–18 months if needed <PIC>. 4. Always verify the float valve is down before closing and locking the lid; it will rise automatically when pressure builds during cooking <PIC>. Never attempt to open the lid while the float valve is up — wait until it drops fully to ensure safe depressurization. For visual guidance, refer to the labeled diagrams of the top and bottom of the pressure cooking lid <PIC><PIC>. , ["lid_top", "lid_bottom", "float_valve_install", "sealing_ring_check", "float_valve_position", "lid_top", "lid_bottom"]

To set the float valve on your Multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Locate the float valve hole on the top of the pressure cooking lid. 2. Drop the narrow end of the float valve into the hole. 3. Turn the lid over and firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. Ensure both the float valve and silicone cap are fully and securely installed before use. Do not operate the appliance without them properly installed. <PIC> , ["pressure_lid_float_valve_install"]

The anti-block shield is a critical safety component for the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer. It is designed to prevent food particles from entering and clogging the steam release pipe, thereby assisting with safe and accurate pressure regulation. It is integral to product safety and mandatory for pressure cooking — the appliance must not be operated without it installed. To install: place the anti-block shield over the prongs on the pressure cooking lid and press down firmly until it snaps securely into position. Before each use, always inspect the anti-block shield (along with the steam release valve, steam release pipe, and float valve) for clogs or damage to ensure safe operation.

To set the steam release valve on your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: 1. Ensure the lid is clean and dry, and confirm the steam release pipe is unobstructed (check anti-block shield and float valve for clogs). 2. Place the steam release valve onto the steam release pipe — it sits loosely but must be pressed down firmly to seat properly <PIC>. 3. When correctly installed, the valve remains in place even when the lid is turned over. 4. Never cover or obstruct the steam release valve — doing so creates a serious safety hazard. 5. Always verify the float valve is down before attempting to open the lid, and never operate the appliance without the steam release valve properly installed. For safe pressure release, refer to your recipe’s recommended method (Natural Release or Quick Release), and never place hands, face, or skin near the valve during venting. , ["pressure_cooker_valve_install"]

To set the anti-block shield on your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: Place the anti-block shield over the prongs on the pressure cooking lid and press down firmly until it snaps securely into position. <PIC> The anti-block shield is essential for safety—it prevents food particles from entering the steam release pipe and supports proper pressure regulation. Never operate the appliance in pressure cooking mode without it installed. To remove it later, grip the lid like a steering wheel and use your thumbs to press firmly against the side of the shield (toward the lid’s edge and upward) until it pops off the prongs. <PIC> , ["shield_install", "shield_removal"]

To set the silicone cap and float valve for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Locate the float valve hole on the top of the pressure cooking lid. 2. Insert the narrow end of the float valve into the hole until it seats securely. 3. Turn the lid over (so the underside faces up), place one finger on the flat top of the float valve to hold it in place, then firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. Ensure both parts are fully seated — the float valve must be installed and capped before any pressure cooking use. ⚠️ Never operate the appliance without both components properly installed, as they work together to seal pressurized steam and ensure safe operation.

To install the condensation collector for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: 1. Locate the condensation collector (a removable tray) and the back of the cooker base. 2. Note the tabs on the back of the cooker base and the matching grooves on the condensation collector. 3. Align the grooves on the condensation collector over the tabs on the cooker base. 4. Gently slide the condensation collector forward into place until fully seated. ✅ It must be installed before cooking. ❗ Remember: The cooker base contains electrical components — keep it dry at all times; never immerse it in water or rinse it under running water.

To install the sealing ring for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: 1. Ensure the sealing ring is clean and slightly damp (wet and soapy makes installation easiest). 2. Place the silicone sealing ring over the circular stainless-steel sealing ring rack on the underside of the pressure cooking lid. 3. Press it firmly and evenly into place all the way around—avoid puckering. 4. When correctly installed, the sealing ring sits snugly behind the rack and will not fall out when the lid is turned upside down. ⚠️ Always check for cuts, deformation, or improper seating before use—and never operate without the sealing ring properly installed. Replace every 12–18 months or sooner if stretched or damaged.

To remove the silicone sealing ring from your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer: Grip the edge of the silicone sealing ring and gently pull it out from behind the circular stainless-steel sealing ring rack. The sealing ring is easiest to remove when it is wet and soapy. After removal, inspect the steel rack to ensure it remains centered and sits at an even height all the way around the lid. Do not attempt to repair a deformed rack. Also, always check the sealing ring for cuts, deformation, or stretching before reuse — replace it every 12–18 months or sooner if damaged.

The silicone sealing ring of the Multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer plays a critical safety and functional role: 1. It creates an airtight seal between the pressure cooking lid and the inner pot during pressure cooking <PIC>. 2. It must be installed correctly before every pressure cooking session — only one sealing ring should be used at a time. 3. It is easiest to remove and install when wet and soapy. To remove: grip the edge of the silicone ring and pull it out from behind the stainless-steel sealing ring rack <PIC>. 4. Always inspect the ring before use for cuts, stretching, deformation, or improper installation — a damaged or stretched ring must not be used <PIC>. 5. Replace the sealing ring every 12–18 months, or sooner if signs of wear appear. 6. Because silicone is porous, it may absorb strong aromas and flavors — keeping extra rings on hand helps prevent flavor transfer between dishes <PIC>. , ["seal001", "seal002", "seal003", "seal004"]

The steam release valve is a critical safety and pressure control component of the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer. It sits loosely on the steam release pipe and works in conjunction with the float valve and silicone cap to seal pressurized steam during cooking <PIC>. When pressure is released (e.g., via Quick Release), steam ejects from the top of the steam release valve — so **never cover it**, and **never place hands, face, or exposed skin over it**, as the steam is extremely hot <PIC>. The valve must be properly installed before use; failure to do so may compromise safety and prevent proper pressure regulation <PIC>. It should be regularly checked for clogging — along with the anti-block shield and steam release pipe — to ensure safe operation <PIC>. To clean: pull the steam release valve straight up and off the pipe, then reinstall securely before next use <PIC>. Note: Do not use Quick Release (QR) when cooking fatty, oily, thick, high-starch, or expanding foods (e.g., beans, rice, congee, stews), as this may cause splattering or blockage <PIC>. , ["drill1_01", "drill1_02", "drill1_03", "drill1_04", "drill1_05", "drill1_06"]

The quick release button (QR or QPR) on the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer controls the steam release valve to safely and rapidly release pressure after cooking. Pressing it down until it clicks and locks into the 'Vent' position stops cooking immediately—ideal for delicate foods like vegetables and seafood to prevent overcooking. Important safety notes: do not turn the button more than 45° (a gentle flick is sufficient), and always let it spring back to its original position. Never operate the button while the float valve is still raised or if the lid is difficult to open—this indicates residual pressure. Always keep hands and face away from the steam release valve during venting to avoid burns. For high-starch or expanding foods (e.g., beans, rice, oatmeal), natural release (NR) is recommended instead of quick release to avoid clogging or splattering.

Natural Release (NR or NPR) is a pressure release method used after pressure cooking in the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer. During NR, the cooker de-pressurizes naturally as the internal temperature gradually drops — cooking stops gradually, and no manual intervention is required. This method is recommended for high-starch foods (e.g., soups, stews, chilis, pasta, oatmeal, congee) and foods that expand during cooking (e.g., beans, grains), as it prevents splattering, foaming, or clogging of the steam release valve. Never attempt to open the lid until the float valve has fully dropped — this confirms all pressure has been safely released. For safety, always follow recipe instructions specifying NR.

您好，关于您询问的 vacuum 机器人结构（robot anatomy），参考文档中虽未提供完整的解剖图或结构术语表，但可归纳其主要物理与功能部件如下： 1. **主体结构**：圆形机身，尺寸约为 144mm（宽）× 105.5mm（高）× 71.5mm（深）<PIC>，重量约 685g（不含电池）； 2. **核心模块**： - 清洁头模块（含可拆卸 extractor 提取器，需按颜色/形状匹配图标安装）<PIC>； - 尘盒（bin）：可取出清空，满尘时自动终止清扫； - 滤网：每次使用后需清洁； 3. **动力系统**：内置锂离子电池（Lithium-ion battery），支持自动回充与断点续扫； 4. **导航与感知**： - 虚拟墙屏障（Dual Mode Virtual Wall Barrier），含 Halo Mode（约24英寸保护圈）<PIC>； - 多传感器协同（通过App接收信息、识别楼梯/障碍物等）； 5. **人机交互**：CLEAN 按钮（带状态指示灯与蜂鸣反馈）、SPOT 按键（定点螺旋清洁）； 6. **充电系统**：Home Base 充电座，支持自动对接与充电。 注：产品无用户可拆卸维修部件，内部电子元件及电池不可自行处理。如需详细结构图，请查阅说明书中的 Figure 1、Figure 2、Figure 3 及 Figure 18（对应虚拟墙、Halo 模式与真空路径示意图）。 , ["Manual36_1"]

The two primary modes available on the vacuum cleaner are: 1. **Auto Mode** — automatically adjusts cleaning behavior based on floor conditions and debris detection (e.g., intensifies cleaning in high-debris areas with forward/backward motion); and 2. **Spot Cleaning Mode** — enables targeted, intensive cleaning of localized debris by spiraling outward ~3 feet in diameter and then inward to the starting point. These modes allow users to tailor performance for general whole-floor cleaning or focused spot treatment.
To properly empty the vacuum cleaner's bin: 1. Press the bin release button to remove the bin. (See Figure 4) , ["Manual32_4"]

For cleaning the vacuum cleaner filter, follow these steps: 1. After each use, empty the dust bin and remove the primary filter. 2. Gently slide the primary filter out of the air-cleaner body — do not knock it against the body. 3. **Do not attempt to clean the primary or safety (inner) filter** — if either is dirty or damaged, both must be replaced. 4. Clean the air-inlet screen and cover, and the inside of the air-cleaner cover (e.g., with compressed air). 5. Reinstall the primary filter carefully, ensuring it is fully seated by pushing on the outer rim — avoid pressing on the soft inner area. 6. Secure the air-cleaner cover with the latches. Note: Never operate the vacuum without both filters and the cover installed.

To clean the full bin sensors on your vacuum cleaner: 1. Remove and empty the dust bin (see Figure 8). 2. Wipe the sensors with a clean, dry cloth — do not spray cleaning solution directly onto the sensors or sensor openings (see Figure 13). , ["Figure 8", "Figure 13"]

The correct procedure for cleaning the extractors (brushes or rollers) on the vacuum cleaner is as follows: 1. Pinch the yellow extractor frame release tabs, lift up the extractor frame, and remove any obstructions. <PIC> 2. Remove hair and debris缠绕 from the extractors themselves. 3. Remove hair and debris from the square and hexagonal plastic pegs on the other side of the extractors. <PIC> 4. Reinstall the extractors, matching the color and shape of the extractor pegs with the color and shape of the extractor icons on the cleaning head module. , ["Figure 15", "Figure 17"]

The proper procedure for cleaning the sensors and charging contacts on the vacuum cleaner is as follows: 1. Wipe the sensor with a clean, dry cloth. Do not spray cleaning solution directly onto the sensors or sensor openings. <PIC> 2. Wipe the charging contacts on the robot (Room ba) and the Home Base with a clean, dry cloth. <PIC> , ["Figure 13", "Figure 14"]

The correct procedure for positioning the Home Base of the vacuum cleaner is as follows: Place the Home Base in an open, uncluttered area with at least 1.5 feet (≈45 cm) of clearance on each side, at least 4 feet (≈122 cm) of clear space in front, and at least 4 feet away from stairs. Additionally, keep it at least 8 feet (≈244 cm) away from virtual wall barriers. Ensure the Home Base is plugged in and positioned in an area with consistent Wi-Fi coverage to support app connectivity and optimal navigation. Avoid placing it near walls, furniture, or obstacles that may block infrared signals or impede docking.

To clean the front caster wheel of your vacuum cleaner, follow this procedure: 1. Pull firmly on the front wheel to remove it (see Figure 11). 2. Once removed, inspect and clear any hair, debris, or dust缠绕 from the wheel and axle. 3. Wipe the wheel and hub with a dry or slightly damp cloth. 4. Reinstall the wheel securely onto the caster fork. Note: For long-term maintenance, greasing the caster-wheel hubs is recommended annually (refer to service instructions), but cleaning should be performed as needed—especially if movement becomes stiff or noisy. <PIC> , ["Figure 11"]

To clean the side brush of your vacuum cleaner, follow these steps: 1. Remove the side brush from the vacuum (typically by pressing a release tab or unscrewing the cover). 2. Rinse the brush thoroughly with warm water to remove hair, dust, and debris. 3. Gently clean the brush post (the shaft it attaches to) as well. 4. Allow the brush to air-dry completely before reinstalling. Note: Always disconnect the vacuum from power and ensure it is turned off before performing maintenance.

The dual-mode virtual wall barrier has two distinct functions depending on the switch position: 1. **Virtual Wall Mode** (switch in 'up' position): Creates an invisible, cone-shaped barrier up to 10 feet wide that prevents the vacuum from crossing designated openings—ideal for blocking doorways or hallways. <PIC> 2. **Halo Mode** (switch in 'down' position): Establishes a protective circular zone (~24 inches radius) around the device, keeping the vacuum from entering sensitive areas like under furniture or near fragile objects (e.g., pet bowls or vases). <PIC> , ["Figure2", "Figure3"]

当真空吸尘器提示问题时，可按以下步骤进行故障排除： 1. 听取提示音与语音信息：吸尘器会发出两声警示音，随后播报具体问题，同时故障指示灯会闪烁； 2. 重启设备：长按 CLEAN 键 10 秒，直至所有指示灯亮起，松开后听到提示音即表示重启成功； 3. 检查并清理吸尘路径（参见图18）<PIC>； 4. 重新安装提取器：确保提取器插销的颜色与形状与清洁头模块上的图标完全匹配； 5. 确保吸尘器放置在开阔、无杂物区域，周围留有足够空间（两侧至少1.5英尺，前方及楼梯前至少4英尺，距虚拟墙屏障至少8英尺）； 6. 检查尘盒是否已满或未正确安装——若尘盒满，吸尘器将完成当前清扫后停止，需清空并重新装入； 7. 确认电池已正确安装且电量充足（出厂预充，但建议及时充电）； 8. 如问题持续，可通过App获取更详细的指导视频与在线支持。 , ["Manual32_18"]

Here are the detailed T-rail mounting instructions for your camera or equipment: 1. **Prepare the mount plate**: Use the included mount plate template — align it using the dashed lines as a guide to set the proper spacing of the T-rail clips for your specific T-rail size (compatible with 9/16", 15/16", or 1-1/2" T-rails). 2. **Mark and verify hole locations**: Before screwing in the mount plate, use the template to determine exact mounting hole positions.
